articles.title: Heatwave eases across Telangana as temperatures dip below seasonal average - Hyderabad Mail
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
articles.full_text:
HYDERABAD: After a prolonged spell of severe heatwave conditions, Telangana witnessed a drop in temperatures over the past two days, bringing relief to residents across the state.
Between April 27 and May 5, maximum temperatures consistently crossed 45°C in north Telangana districts, while Hyderabad recorded temperatures above 42°C during the period.
Since May 6, temperatures have declined across several regions. North and east Telangana districts recorded around 42°C, while Hyderabad’s maximum temperature fell to nearly 39°C.
Weather conditions are expected to remain favourable over the next three to four days, with temperatures likely to stay below normal levels. Scattered thunderstorms are also forecast across parts of the state, providing further respite from the heatwave conditions.
The fall in temperatures has come as a relief for residents after nearly 10 days of intense summer heat across Telangana.

articles.title: AIG Hospitals launches ‘Beat the Heat’ campaign to raise heatstroke awareness in Telangana - Telangana Today
articles.search_term: (heatstroke OR "heat stroke" OR sunstroke OR "heat exhaustion" OR "heat-related death" OR "heat casualty" OR "heat fatality" OR "suspected heat death" OR dehydration OR "heat cramps" OR "heat illness") Telangana
articles.full_text:
Home |Hyderabad |Aig Hospitals Launches Beat The Heat Campaign To Raise Heatstroke Awareness In Telangana
AIG Hospitals launches ‘Beat the Heat’ campaign to raise heatstroke awareness in Telangana
AIG Hospitals has launched ‘Beat the Heat’ in Hyderabad to educate citizens on heatstroke prevention, symptoms and first aid amid rising summer temperatures in Telangana. The initiative focuses on awareness camps, expert talks and practical guidance for emergency response.
Hyderabad: With Telangana witnessing intense summer conditions, AIG Hospitals, Banjara Hills has launched ‘Beat the Heat,’ a public awareness initiative to educate citizens on heatstroke, heat-related illnesses, early warning signs, first-aid measures, and preventive action.
Though intermittent rainfall and thunderstorms may bring temporary relief, weather advisories continue to underline the need for caution as heat stress remains a serious public health concern, AIG hospitals said.
As part of the initiative, AIG Hospitals, Banjara Hills will conduct public awareness camps, doctor-led talks, provide educational materials, conduct media outreach, engage the community, and offer practical guidance on recognising and responding to heat-related illness.
Important precautions:
Red flags of heatstrokes:
Tired, dizzy, nauseous, headache, muscle cramps, confusion, unusual behaviour, slurred speech, fainting, persistent vomiting, seizures, or not sweating despite extreme heat should never be ignored.
What to do?
Start cooling the body, arrange medical help, move to shade/ air-conditioned area, remove excess clothing, apply cool water or wet cloths, fan the person, and place ice packs or cool towels around the neck, armpits, and groin.
If the person is conscious, small sips of water or oral rehydration can be given. But if the person is confused, drowsy, vomiting, or unconscious, do not give anything orally because of the risk of choking.
Cooling should begin even before the ambulance arrives, because in heat stroke, cooling is the treatment.

articles.title: Hyderabad rains to ease heatwave pressure - urbanacres.in
articles.full_text:
A forecast of widespread rainfall across Telangana is expected to bring a temporary drop in temperatures, offering relief to Hyderabad and surrounding districts after weeks of extreme heat. The shift, driven by evolving weather systems, could lower daytime temperatures by up to 5°C over the coming days, marking a significant pause in the ongoing heatwave that has strained urban infrastructure and public health systems.
According to meteorological assessments, thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds are likely across multiple districts, including Hyderabad. The cooling effect is expected to keep maximum temperatures below 39°C for several days, compared to recent highs exceeding 42°C in the city and over 45°C in parts of the state. This weather shift comes after a prolonged period of intense heat that pushed electricity demand, water consumption, and health risks to critical levels. Urban centres such as Hyderabad have seen rising energy use due to cooling needs, exposing the vulnerability of city systems to climate extremes. The expected rainfall may ease immediate pressure, but experts caution that such fluctuations reflect deeper climate variability rather than a stable seasonal transition.Meteorologists attribute the incoming rain to a trough system influencing weather patterns across central and southern India. Official forecasts indicate light to moderate rainfall activity persisting for several days, with thunderstorms and wind speeds reaching 30–40 kmph in some areas.
For cities, the implications extend beyond temperature relief. Sudden pre-monsoon rainfall can disrupt traffic flows, strain drainage systems, and expose gaps in urban flood preparedness. Hyderabad, like many fast-growing cities, faces recurring challenges in managing short bursts of intense rainfall due to uneven infrastructure upgrades and rapid land-use changes.Urban planners note that such weather events highlight the need for climate-responsive infrastructure — including improved stormwater drainage, heat-resilient building design, and better early warning systems. While rainfall offers short-term respite, it does little to address longer-term concerns such as groundwater depletion and uneven water distribution, both of which have been exacerbated by erratic weather cycles.The agricultural hinterland around Hyderabad is also on alert, as unseasonal rains combined with strong winds can damage standing crops and harvested produce. This creates a ripple effect on urban food supply chains and market prices, reinforcing how closely city economies are tied to regional climate patterns.
As Telangana transitions into the pre-monsoon phase, the current rainfall episode underscores a broader trend: cities must adapt to increasingly unpredictable weather. For Hyderabad, balancing rapid urban growth with climate resilience will be central to managing both heat stress and sudden rainfall events in the years ahead.

articles.title: Hyderabad gets respite from heatwave, IMD forecasts rains till May 10 - The Siasat Daily
articles.search_term: heatwave (Adilabad OR "Bhadradri Kothagudem" OR Hyderabad OR Jagtial OR Jangaon OR "Jayashankar Bhoopalpally" OR "Jogulamba Gadwal" OR Kamareddy OR Karimnagar OR Khammam OR "Komaram Bheem Asifabad" OR Mahabubabad OR Mahabubnagar OR Mancherial OR Medak OR Medchal OR Nagarkurnool OR Nalgonda OR Nirmal OR Nizamabad OR Peddapalli OR "Rajanna Sircilla" OR Rangareddy OR Sangareddy OR Siddipet OR Suryapet OR Vikarabad OR Wanaparthy OR "Warangal (Rural)" OR "Warangal (Urban)" OR "Yadadri Bhuvanagiri") Telangana
articles.full_text:
Hyderabad: Residents of Hyderabad who were witnessing an intense heatwave for the past few days finally got some respite as temperatures declined and the India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast rains till May 10.
The temperatures that crossed 42 degrees Celsius in Hyderabad and 45 degrees Celsius in other districts of Telangana have declined.
Temperatures declined below 35 degrees Celsius
The lowest maximum temperature of 35 degrees Celsius recorded yesterday was in Nagarkurnool district.
In the case of Hyderabad, the lowest temperature was 36 degrees Celsius, which was recorded in Khairatabad.
Even the highest maximum temperature recorded in Nizamabad district was 42.8 degrees Celsius.
Meanwhile, the IMD Hyderabad has forecast rains in the state till May 10. It has also forecast thunderstorms, lightning, squalls, etc. till Sunday.
A weather enthusiast, T Balaji, who is known for his accurate forecasts, wrote on his X handle, Telangana Weatherman, “The temperatures during April 27 to May 5 were continuously touching 45°C+ in North TG and 42°C+ in Hyderabad.
Since yesterday, the heatwave has reduced with 42°C+ in North, East TG and 39°C in Hyderabad.
This is good respite from heatwave, below normal temperatures to continue for next 3-4 days with scattered thunderstorms.”
IMD forecasts rains, hazy conditions in Hyderabad
Apart from rains, the city is expected to witness hazy conditions during the morning hours till May 11.
The city is also likely to witness a cloudy sky.
In view of the expected weather conditions forecast by IMD Hyderabad, residents need to plan their travel accordingly.
